criteria_results.notes: src/app/api/repos/[repoId]/route.ts exists. It checks for X-Reporter-Token header (returns 401 if absent), validates the token against execution_logs.reporter_token, then verifies the execution's feature request is linked to the requested repoId before returning repo data.
server.ts registers exactly 6 tools via server.tool(): get_feature_request, send_execution_event, get_execution_status, poll_answer, acknowledge_execution, get_repo.
cli.ts registers 6 subcommands: get-feature-request, send-event, get-status, poll-answer, acknowledge, get-repo. Each maps 1-to-1 to a client function and a MCP tool.

The branch introduces 9 new lint errors (1153 total vs 1144 on master), all @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any violations in the new test files. Specifically: cli.test.ts lines 39, 76, 93, 120, 150, 164 and server.test.ts lines 44, 88, 170 all use 'as any' casts. The project's ESLint config treats no-explicit-any as an error. The pre-existing lint failures are on master and are not this branch's responsibility, but the net new errors introduced here are.

evidence.command: # master: 1144 problems (179 errors); feat/mcp: 1153 problems (188 errors) — delta: +9 errors
evidence.stdout_snippet: master: 179 errors | feat/mcp: 188 errors | new errors: 9

findings.description: Six uses of 'as any' cast on mock return values (lines 39, 76, 93, 120, 150, 164). ESLint @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any is configured as error.
findings.suggestion: Replace 'as any' with typed return values matching the actual function return types (e.g., 'as PendingExecutionResponse', 'as ExecutionStatusResponse', etc.) imported from client.ts.

findings.description: Three uses of 'as any' cast on vi.mocked return values (lines 44, 88, 170). Same no-explicit-any violation.
findings.suggestion: Use the concrete return types from client.ts. For example, line 44: mockResolvedValue(mockData as PendingExecutionResponse).

findings.description: process.exit mock uses 'as any' to cast the mock implementation (lines 76, 120, 164). This is the same no-explicit-any rule.
findings.suggestion: Type the mock as 'never': mockImplementation((() => { throw new Error('process.exit called') }) as (...args: Parameters<typeof process.exit>) => never).

findings.description: Error messages are in German ('X-Reporter-Token ist erforderlich', 'Ungültiger Token', etc.) while the rest of the API routes use English. This is an inconsistency in convention.
findings.suggestion: Align language with other routes or establish an explicit convention. Not a blocker.

findings.description: The isMainModule guard checks for process.argv[1] ending with /server.ts in addition to /server.js. After compilation, only /server.js will ever be present at runtime; the .ts check is dead code.
findings.suggestion: Remove the .ts suffix check from the isMainModule condition. Minor cosmetic issue.

summary: 7 of 8 criteria pass. All planned files exist, mcp:build compiles cleanly, all 38 tests pass (18 client + 11 server + 9 CLI), the repos/[repoId] route has correct X-Reporter-Token auth, all 6 MCP tools are registered, and all 6 CLI subcommands are present. QF-08 fails: the branch introduces 9 net-new @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any lint errors (6 in cli.test.ts, 3 in server.test.ts) against a project config that treats this rule as an error. These are fixable with typed casts using the existing return types from client.ts.
